data.epi_content:
He no longer shut himself in his room like before.

He would show his face in class just enough to avoid expulsion from the Academy, then return to his room to continue his research, forming hypotheses in his mind and repeating thought experiments.

These were experiments to determine how much magical power could be extracted with sufficient time, and how to extract magical power as quickly as possible while killing the subject.

The only problem was that he lacked test subjects.

After Erica burned to death, letters had arrived from his family saying things like "the family is struggling" and "what have you done," but he paid them no mind and tossed them into the fireplace to burn.

It wasn't particularly important information anyway.

The family probably wouldn't collapse during his lifetime.

Just before he met Vivian and died, he had encountered a familiar-faced hound sent by his family.

Vivian had completely lost her mind since that day.

The only sounds coming from her room were constant crying or hoarse, cracked voices, so Evan didn't bother visiting her.

Vivian would overcome this.

In a somewhat different way.

For some reason, his future self seemed to resent Vivian quite a bit.

Thoughts about needing to kill her or her not being helpful came to mind, but for now, he decided to leave her be.

If Vivian acquired more knowledge and learned more spells, she would certainly be of great help in saving Erica.

At least when it came to spellcasting, Vivian was far superior to Evan.

While Evan was merely a prodigy who quickly mastered existing knowledge, Vivian was a genius who could create new things.

He had sent a note under her door asking her to visit today, so she would probably come.

Lately, she had started talking to others again, using spells little by little, and greeting the Crown Prince and other students.

Or well, he could just wait a little longer.

Anyway, there was a more important matter at hand.

Today, after a long time of having knowledge but no test subjects, he had finally obtained some quality specimens.

"Your name was Lydia, right?"

"Mmph! Mmph!"

Lydia was trying desperately to scream with a white cloth stuffed in her mouth, surrounded by her unconscious followers.

But the gag wasn't going to disappear just because she tried to scream.

People tend to forget common sense and basic concepts when they're desperate.

"It's hard to find test subjects inside the Academy.

But you made things much easier by coming to this desolate place to bully others, just like you bullied Erica."

Evan approached Lydia and removed the cloth gagging her mouth.

"If you scream, I'll turn you to dust."

"O-okay, I won't scream."

"Haha. It's not that you shouldn't scream—it's that I'll kill you if you do."

Evan clicked his tongue like an old man, disapproving of how she still didn't understand her position as a test subject, and began gathering magical power at his fingertips.

Then, he unclenched his right fist.

Simultaneously, as the magical power spread out, the slightly plump female student who had been lying next to Lydia transformed into a slimy mass.

Above it, a slightly cloudy orb began to form.

"The quality isn't very good."

Evan approached the orb with familiarity, picked it up, and made his assessment.

Soon, the orb began to be absorbed into Evan's body.
data.epi_content2:
"The heart inside is twisted and disgusting."

Lydia wet herself watching this.

The unpleasant smell of urine would normally have risen, but fortunately—or unfortunately—it was masked by the stench coming from the melted mass.

A black mage who had been evil since childhood, who didn't see people as people, and who cast curses that burned even the soul.

Lydia nearly fainted from fear.

Until just moments ago, she had been bonding with her companions while finding suitable prey to bully, and now one of the girls had melted into something like flour dough.

No sane person could maintain their composure in such a situation.

Though she was a knight's daughter, the girl had been strong and obedient to Lydia's whims, so they had been quite close.

Such a person had died instantly, treated worse than a dog.

"P-please spare me. Please let me live."

Hearing this, Evan clicked his tongue and replied.

"Living would be more painful. That friend next to you is still alive, you know."

Looking at the mass after hearing those words, it was indeed moving like a slime, literally still alive.

When Lydia was about to scream "Eek!", he cast a spell that stuffed an ice chunk into her mouth and approached the squelching mass of flesh.

Then he stomped on it, and with the sound of flesh being crushed, the movement stopped.

"What? Do you still want to live even after seeing this?"

Lydia couldn't nod or shake her head.

Only a sense of injustice rose in her heart, wondering why she had to suffer such a terrible fate.

She didn't want to die, but if she lived, she would have to become something as horrific as that.

Wasn't he basically saying he would kill them both anyway?

However, she was aware that she had quite useful connections behind her.

So she dared to make an impossible proposal to the black mage.

"If you just let me go unharmed, I'll give you everything—my family's support, or even marriage and all my assets!

Afterward, you can divorce me and throw me away, just please spare my—"

Her words were cut short.

Evan had kicked Lydia, who was sitting down, in the solar plexus.

She made choking sounds and couldn't breathe properly, clutching her chest in vain.

After her breathing stabilized somewhat following the nausea, allowing her to speak again, Evan asked her a question.

"How did you bully Erica?"

"E-E-who?"

"Duke Mecklenburg's daughter."

"No, ah. You're bringing up something from years ago...mmph!!"

He grabbed the test subject by the hair and shoved her face into the melted mass of flesh beside her.

Even just dipping one's face in water that had been stagnant for a week would be torturous due to the smell, so Evan wondered what it felt like to have one's face shoved into a mass of freshly melted human flesh.

Lydia could only express her sense of injustice.

To her, Erica was nothing more than a slightly unusual bullying target.

Though she had increased the severity of her bullying toward other students after Erica's death.

"Did I do something like this to you before?

Ah, no. Back then, Erica killed you.

It's my memory but not my memory. It's a bit confusing.

I don't know. I just don't know."
data.epi_content3:
Lydia felt she might go insane from the fear she felt watching the madman spouting nonsense before her.

"The girl I just melted had a body like a knight's, so I thought she'd be full of energy, but she was garbage."

He spoke as if he had gone to a restaurant with high expectations, paid a lot of money, and left after eating tasteless food.

Evan's mood was essentially similar to that.

"You've probably lived happily, eating good food and living in nice places, so you should be suitable for any experiment. I'll give you a choice!"

"W-what's the choice?"

Instead of answering, Evan took out a potion from his pocket, shook it, and then pointed at the squelching mass of flesh next to Lydia.

Lydia pointed at the potion with a trembling finger.

He shook the potion for quite a while before tossing it to Lydia.

"Drink it."

"...What will happen if I drink it?"

"I don't know either, that's why I'm telling you to drink it."

Lydia was about to refuse, but after meeting Evan's gaze—which looked at her as if she were an inanimate object—she decided not to open her mouth, her hands trembling.

She opened the potion bottle, sniffed it once, realized it had no scent, and drank it all.

At first, nothing happened.

About a minute passed.

Then, like a freshly infected zombie, she began twisting her body grotesquely, her joints making cracking sounds as they contorted.

Scales, needles, and fins began to grow on her arms.

Strangely, her face maintained its original form.

"Uh, uh. Uh. Guh. Uh. What, what is this, what's happening?"

She stuck out her tongue, opening her mouth as wide as possible as if trying to taste more air.

When her mouth was opened to its limit, Evan grabbed it and tore it apart.

Contrary to his expectations, no dramatic monster was created.

Her tongue just became slightly sharper and split into two.

"What? Why isn't she transforming?

I don't know, I'll have to lock her up and observe her slowly later."

He placed the mass of flesh and the grotesquely transformed, choking Lydia into a black box, closed the lid, and began chanting some spell.

After the box glowed slightly, Evan took out a small crystal from inside.

Within it, a tiny figure resembling Lydia was knocking on the walls, as if asking to be let out.

"I spared your life, so stay still before I shatter this."

Evan lifted the crystal and began compressing it slightly with magical power at his fingertips.

The contents inside immediately fell backward and collapsed.

Evan put the crystal in his pocket and began his experiments again.

Of the four people he had captured, one had melted, one had her soul trapped in a crystal along with the melted one, and as he was about to fuse the remaining two to extract magical power, there was a knock at the door.

"Evan, it's me. Did you read the letter I left under your room?"

"It's not locked, just open it and come in."

Vivian entered the room.

At the same time, her eyes widened as she saw the bound children who looked like they were dead.

"Evan, what is this? What are you doing right now...?"
